---
title: "ML for Remote Sensing (ICLR 2023)"
description: "Insights and resources from the Machine Learning for Remote Sensing Workshop at ICLR 2023."
toc: true
include-in-header: 
  text: |
    <script src="../assets/js/system-init.js"></script>
---

# Machine Learning for Remote Sensing Workshop
## ICLR 2023 | Kigali, Rwanda

The Machine Learning for Remote Sensing workshop at ICLR 2023 focused on the advancements in using Earth observation data for addressing global challenges such as climate change, food security, and natural disasters. Remote sensing datasets present unique challenges, including high dimensionality and complex spatial-temporal-spectral dynamics.

## Core Research Topics

The workshop solicited research addressing several critical areas in the field:

- **Domain Adaptation**: Methods for handling geographical and temporal shifts in data distribution.
- **Model Architectures**: Designing neural networks specifically optimized for multi-modal remote sensing data.
- **Geographic Equity**: Emphasis on research and solutions tailored to the African context.
- **Semi-Supervised Learning**: Techniques for leveraging large amounts of unlabeled satellite imagery.

## Accepted Research Papers

Select highlights from the workshop submissions:

| Category | Research Paper |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Land Cover Mapping** | [Towards Explainable Land Cover Mapping: a Counterfactual-based Strategy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nasaharvest/ml-for-remote-sensing/main/iclr2023/papers/10/CameraReady/2023_CounterFactualSITS_ICLR_workshop_Camera-ready.pdf) |
| **Acoustic Classification** | [Enhancing Acoustic Classification using Meta-Data](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nasaharvest/ml-for-remote-sensing/main/iclr2023/papers/43/CameraReady/Enhancing_Acoustic_Classification_ICLR_2023_Jeantet_Dufourq.pdf) |
| **Poverty Prediction** | [Urban-rural disparities in satellite-based poverty prediction](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nasaharvest/ml-for-remote-sensing/main/iclr2023/papers/35/CameraReady/ICLRCameraReady.pdf) |
| **Building Delineation** | [Polygonizer: An auto-regressive building delineator](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2304.04048.pdf) |
| **Yield Forecasting** | [State-Level Wheat Yield Forecasts in Kazakhstan](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nasaharvest/ml-for-remote-sensing/main/iclr2023/papers/38/CameraReady/ICLR_1st_Remote_Sensing_Workshop_CameraReady.pdf) |

## Distinguished Speakers

- **Abigail Annkah** (Google AI): Research Software Engineer.
- **Joyce Nakatumba-Nabende** (Makerere AI Lab): Head of AI Lab, Makerere University.
- **Kristof Van Tricht** (VITO): Remote Sensing Scientist.

## Resources and External Links

- [Official Workshop Website](https://nasaharvest.github.io/ml-for-remote-sensing/iclr2023/)
- [NASA Harvest GitHub Repository](https://github.com/nasaharvest)
- [ICLR 2023 Program](https://iclr.cc/Conferences/2023/Schedule)

---
*Note: This summary is curated for academic reference based on the ICLR 2023 ML4RS Workshop materials.*
